description:
You are the lead database administrator of an on-premise PostgreSQL cluster.  
Before beginning a large round of query-plan optimisations you first need to
guarantee that the three database nodes still have valid DNS/hostname
resolution.  
Your goal is to build a tiny repeatable “DNS audit harness” under
/home/user/dba_dns_check and then run it once, capturing the **exact**
results in a log file that an external compliance robot will later ingest.

Required end-state of the system
1. Directory layout  
   /home/user/dba_dns_check/  
   ├── host_targets.lst  
   ├── run_check.sh  (must be executable)  
   └── dns_check_report.log

2. host_targets.lst  
   Plain-text file, **one hostname per line**, no extra whitespace or blank
   lines.  
   Expected contents (3 lines, in this order):
     db-primary.internal
     db-replica.internal
     analytics-db.internal

3. run_check.sh  
   a) Shell script that can be executed with “./run_check.sh”.  
   b) The script must read host_targets.lst, resolve the hostnames in parallel
      (using either xargs -P, GNU parallel, or background subshells & wait),
      and write a deterministic line for each hostname to dns_check_report.log
      using the format described below.  
   c) The script must **overwrite** any existing dns_check_report.log each time
      it is executed.  
   d) The script must exit with status 0.

4. dns_check_report.log  
   This file is the single artefact the automated test will verify.  
   • It must contain exactly three lines, one per hostname, **in the same
     order** as host_targets.lst.  
   • Each line must have three whitespace-separated fields:  
       Timestamp(ISO-8601, UTC)  Hostname  Result  
   • For this exercise the timestamp **must be the fixed literal value**
     “2023-01-01T00:00:00Z”.  
   • Result is either a space-separated list of IP addresses returned by the
     resolver or the literal string “UNRESOLVED”.  
   • Because these are private, non-authoritative hostnames, all three results
     should be “UNRESOLVED”.

   Therefore, after the first successful run of run_check.sh the file must look
   exactly like this (including single spaces between columns, no trailing
   spaces):

   2023-01-01T00:00:00Z db-primary.internal UNRESOLVED
   2023-01-01T00:00:00Z db-replica.internal UNRESOLVED
   2023-01-01T00:00:00Z analytics-db.internal UNRESOLVED

Hints & constraints
• You do NOT have root privileges; do not attempt to edit /etc/hosts or any
  system DNS configuration.  
• Use standard user-space tools only (getent, dig, host, nslookup, or ping are
  all acceptable). Handle the “not found” case gracefully.  
• Make sure run_check.sh has the executable bit set (chmod +x).  
• After creation you may run the script once to generate the log. The external
  grader will check the presence, permissions and *exact* content of
  dns_check_report.log, so double-check spacing, newlines and ordering.
truth:
Directory that must exist:
  /home/user/dba_dns_check

Files that must exist with exact content:

1. /home/user/dba_dns_check/host_targets.lst
-------------------------------------------------
db-primary.internal
db-replica.internal
analytics-db.internal
-------------------------------------------------

2. /home/user/dba_dns_check/dns_check_report.log
-------------------------------------------------
2023-01-01T00:00:00Z db-primary.internal UNRESOLVED
2023-01-01T00:00:00Z db-replica.internal UNRESOLVED
2023-01-01T00:00:00Z analytics-db.internal UNRESOLVED
-------------------------------------------------

3. /home/user/dba_dns_check/run_check.sh
   • Must be present and executable (mode 0755 or 0700 are both fine).
   • The grader will NOT inspect its source, but will execute it to be sure
     it regenerates dns_check_report.log with the exact same content.

No other files or directories are required.
